title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. lanatum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. lanatum, commonly known as wild angelica or cow parsley, is a biennial plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Heracleum sphondylium subsp. lanatum lie in its essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in the production of essential oils and creams for topical use.
